evals.prompt: I need to add digital signature functionality to my Express app. Set up TurboDocx so I can send documents for signing and check their status.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboSign config file, route handlers for sendSignature and getStatus, wires routes into the main app, adds .env with TurboSign vars, installs @turbodocx/sdk

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created

assertions.name: config-has-turbosign
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboSign.configure with API key and sender email env vars

assertions.name: route-file-created
assertions.description: A route/handler file for signatures was created

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to import and register signature routes

assertions.name: env-file-created
assertions.description: .env file contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: gitignore-has-env
assertions.description: .gitignore includes .env

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY (user only asked for TurboSign)

assertions.name: uses-typescript
assertions.description: Generated files use .ts extension (tsconfig.json was detected)

assertions.name: esm-imports
assertions.description: Generated code uses ESM import syntax (package.json type is module)

evals.prompt: Set up TurboDocx TurboPartner in my FastAPI app so I can provision and manage customer organizations.

assertions.name: config-file-created

assertions.name: config-has-turbopartner
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboPartner.configure with partner API key and partner ID env vars

assertions.name: router-file-created

assertions.name: router-wired
assertions.description: app/main.py was modified to include the new router

assertions.name: env-has-partner-vars

assertions.name: no-sign-vars

assertions.name: uses-fastapi-patterns

evals.prompt: I need both TurboSign and TurboPartner in my Go project. Set up the TurboDocx SDK with both products.
evals.expected_output: Creates config file with both TurboSign client and TurboPartner client, handler files, wires into main.go, adds .env with all 6 vars

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A Go config/client file was created for TurboDocx

assertions.name: config-has-both-clients
assertions.description: Config file creates both NewClientWithConfig (TurboSign) and NewPartnerClient (TurboPartner)

assertions.name: handler-files-created
assertions.description: Handler files were created for signature and/or partner operations

assertions.name: main-wired
assertions.description: cmd/server/main.go was modified to import and register handlers

assertions.name: env-has-all-vars
assertions.description: .env contains all 6 vars: TURBODOCX_API_KEY, ORG_ID, SENDER_EMAIL, SENDER_NAME, PARTNER_API_KEY, PARTNER_ID

assertions.name: uses-context
assertions.description: Handler code uses context.Context for SDK calls (Go convention)

assertions.name: follows-project-structure

assertions.name: controller-created

assertions.name: module-wired

evals.prompt: Set up TurboDocx in my Express app so I can generate documents from a template by passing in variable values. I have a contract template in TurboDocx and want to programmatically render it for each customer.

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A Deliverable config/client file was created

assertions.name: config-has-deliverable

assertions.name: no-sender-config
assertions.description: Deliverable.configure does NOT pass senderEmail/senderName (those are TurboSign-only)

assertions.name: route-file-created
assertions.description: A route/handler file for deliverable generation was created

assertions.name: uses-generateDeliverable
assertions.description: Route handler calls Deliverable.generateDeliverable with templateId + variables

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to import and register deliverable routes

assertions.name: env-has-shared-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_ORG_ID

assertions.name: no-sender-env-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL (Deliverable doesn't need sender vars)

assertions.name: no-partner-vars

assertions.name: gitignore-has-env
assertions.description: .gitignore includes .env

evals.prompt: I'm building a contract automation flow in my Next.js App Router app. When a user submits a form, I need to generate a contract from a TurboDocx template, then immediately route it for e-signature. Set everything up.
evals.expected_output: Creates a config file with both Deliverable and TurboSign clients, an App Router API route that calls Deliverable.generateDeliverable then TurboSign.sendSignature with the returned deliverableId, adds .env with shared API key/org + sender vars

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboDocx config/client file was created

assertions.name: config-has-both-modules
assertions.description: Config file configures both Deliverable AND TurboSign

assertions.name: api-route-created
assertions.description: API route handler created in app/api/ directory using Next.js App Router conventions (route.ts)

assertions.name: calls-generateDeliverable
assertions.description: Route handler calls Deliverable.generateDeliverable

assertions.name: uses-deliverableId-not-buffer
assertions.description: Route handler passes deliverableId to TurboSign.sendSignature (does NOT download the generated document and re-upload as a file/Buffer)

assertions.name: uses-route-handlers
assertions.description: API routes export named functions (POST) — Next.js App Router pattern

assertions.name: env-has-shared-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_ORG_ID

assertions.name: env-has-sender-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL (required by TurboSign)

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

assertions.name: no-app-wiring
assertions.description: No attempt to wire routes into a main app file (Next.js uses file-based routing)

evals.prompt: Add the ability to my Express app to cancel a signature request with a reason (e.g. counterparty wanted revisions) and to resend reminder emails to specific recipients who haven't signed yet.
evals.expected_output: Creates endpoints calling TurboSign.void(documentId, reason) and TurboSign.resend(documentId, recipientIds) with the correct names and parameter types

assertions.name: uses-void-correct-name
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboSign.void (the correct method name) — NOT TurboSign.voidDocument

assertions.name: passes-reason-arg
assertions.description: TurboSign.void is called with a reason argument (second parameter)

assertions.name: uses-resend-correct-name
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboSign.resend (the correct method name) — NOT TurboSign.resendEmail

assertions.name: passes-recipient-ids-array
assertions.description: TurboSign.resend is called with an array of recipient IDs/UUIDs — NOT a single recipient email string

assertions.name: no-obsolete-names
assertions.description: Code does NOT contain TurboSign.voidDocument or TurboSign.resendEmail (those are wrong names from the old skill)

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to register the new routes

assertions.name: env-has-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

evals.prompt: Once a signature is completed, I want users to download the signed PDF directly from my Express app. Add a download endpoint that streams the file back with the right Content-Type header so the browser downloads it as a PDF.
evals.expected_output: Creates a download endpoint that calls TurboSign.download(documentId), correctly converts the Blob to a Node Buffer, and streams it with application/pdf Content-Type

assertions.name: uses-download-method
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboSign.download(documentId)

assertions.name: handles-blob-correctly
assertions.description: Code calls .arrayBuffer() on the result (or otherwise correctly handles Blob — does NOT treat result as a Node Buffer directly which would be wrong)

assertions.name: no-direct-buffer-assumption
assertions.description: Code does NOT directly do `const buffer: Buffer = await TurboSign.download(...)` or `(await TurboSign.download(...)).pipe(...)` — these patterns assume Buffer/stream and would fail at runtime since download returns Blob

assertions.name: sets-pdf-content-type
assertions.description: Response sets Content-Type to application/pdf

assertions.name: sets-content-disposition
assertions.description: Response sets Content-Disposition header so browser downloads as a file (attachment or inline)

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to register the download route

assertions.name: env-has-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

evals.prompt: I'm a TurboDocx partner building an admin dashboard for my customers' onboarding. I need three things in my Express app: (1) provision a new customer organization, (2) invite a user to that newly-created org with a 'contributor' role, and (3) issue an org-scoped API key for the new org. Set everything up.
evals.expected_output: Creates TurboPartner config + endpoints for createOrganization, addUserToOrganization, createOrganizationApiKey — and surfaces the API key value (only returned once) in the response

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboPartner config/client file was created

assertions.name: calls-createOrganization
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboPartner.createOrganization

assertions.name: calls-addUserToOrganization
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboPartner.addUserToOrganization with an email and role

assertions.name: uses-contributor-role
assertions.description: addUserToOrganization is called with role 'contributor' (matching the user request)

assertions.name: calls-createOrganizationApiKey
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboPartner.createOrganizationApiKey

assertions.name: surfaces-api-key-value
assertions.description: Response returns or surfaces the key field from the createOrganizationApiKey response (.data.key) — this is critical because the full key value is only returned on creation

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to register the new routes

assertions.name: env-has-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_PARTNER_ID

assertions.name: no-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL (partner-only, no signatures requested)

evals.prompt: Set up TurboDocx in my Express app. I need to upload a contract PDF, place signature fields on it, and then show the user a preview/review URL so they can verify the field placement BEFORE the document is actually sent for signature. Once they confirm the preview looks right, that's when I'll send it.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboSign config file, route handlers for both createSignatureReviewLink (preview, no emails sent) AND sendSignature (final send), exposes previewUrl from the review response, wires routes into the main app

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboSign config/client file was created

assertions.name: uses-createSignatureReviewLink
assertions.description: Route handler calls TurboSign.createSignatureReviewLink (the preview-without-sending method)

assertions.name: surfaces-previewUrl
assertions.description: Route handler returns or surfaces the previewUrl field from the createSignatureReviewLink response

assertions.name: separate-from-send
assertions.description: Preview endpoint exists at a different path than the send endpoint (e.g. /review and /send) — the user wants two-step flow

assertions.name: uses-sendSignature-for-final
assertions.description: A separate sendSignature endpoint exists for the final send step (this is the two-step preview-then-send workflow)

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to import and register the routes

assertions.name: env-has-sign-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

evals.prompt: Set up TurboDocx webhooks in my Express app so I can receive real-time signature.document.completed events instead of polling. I need (1) an endpoint that receives the webhook POST and verifies the X-TurboDocx-Signature header, and (2) an admin endpoint that registers the signature webhook with TurboDocx so my receiver URL gets called.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboWebhooks config (admin-scoped, NO senderEmail), an unauthenticated POST /webhooks/turbodocx receiver that reads the RAW body and calls verifyWebhookSignature before trusting the payload, and an admin endpoint that calls TurboWebhooks.createWebhook and surfaces the secret (shown once). Adds .env with TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET — no sender email, no partner vars.

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboWebhooks config/client file was created

assertions.name: config-uses-webhooks-configure
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboWebhooks.configure (or imports and configures the TurboWebhooks module) with apiKey and orgId — NOT TurboSign.configure

assertions.name: no-sender-email-in-config
assertions.description: TurboWebhooks.configure is NOT called with senderEmail/senderName — webhook routes don't send email and the SDK skips that validation

assertions.name: receiver-route-created
assertions.description: An HTTP route file was created for the inbound webhook receiver (e.g. POST /webhooks/turbodocx)

assertions.name: uses-raw-body
assertions.description: Receiver route uses express.raw({ type: 'application/json' }) middleware (or otherwise captures the raw request body as a Buffer) — required because the HMAC is computed over exact bytes

assertions.name: calls-verifyWebhookSignature
assertions.description: Receiver calls verifyWebhookSignature (the free function imported from @turbodocx/sdk) with the raw body, X-TurboDocx-Signature header, X-TurboDocx-Timestamp header, and the secret

assertions.name: no-json-parse-before-verify
assertions.description: Code does NOT use express.json() / JSON.parse / req.body as a parsed object BEFORE verifying the signature — that would lose whitespace and break HMAC

assertions.name: calls-createWebhook
assertions.description: An admin endpoint calls TurboWebhooks.createWebhook with HTTPS urls and an events array

assertions.name: surfaces-secret-once
assertions.description: Response from the create endpoint exposes the secret field (shown ONCE — must be captured by the caller)

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to register the webhook receiver and admin routes

assertions.name: env-has-webhook-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, and TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET

assertions.name: no-sender-env-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL (TurboWebhooks does not send mail — this is a common confusion with TurboSign)

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

assertions.name: gitignore-has-env
assertions.description: .gitignore includes .env

evals.prompt: Add TurboDocx webhook support to my FastAPI app. I need an endpoint that receives signature.document.completed deliveries from TurboDocx and verifies the X-TurboDocx-Signature header, plus a way to register the webhook on startup.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboWebhooks Python config (admin-scoped, NO sender email), a FastAPI route that reads the raw request body and calls verify_webhook_signature, and an endpoint or startup helper that calls TurboWebhooks.create_webhook. Adds .env with TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboWebhooks config file was created

assertions.name: config-uses-webhooks-configure
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboWebhooks.configure with api_key and org_id

assertions.name: no-sender-email-in-config
assertions.description: TurboWebhooks.configure is NOT called with sender_email/sender_name

assertions.name: receiver-route-created
assertions.description: A FastAPI router file was created for the inbound webhook receiver

assertions.name: uses-raw-body
assertions.description: Receiver reads the raw request body via `await request.body()` (returns bytes) — NOT a parsed Pydantic model or `await request.json()`

assertions.name: calls-verify-webhook-signature
assertions.description: Receiver calls verify_webhook_signature from the turbodocx package with the raw body bytes, X-TurboDocx-Signature header, X-TurboDocx-Timestamp header, and the secret

assertions.name: no-pydantic-before-verify
assertions.description: Receiver signature does NOT take a Pydantic BaseModel / parsed dict parameter BEFORE verification — that would let FastAPI re-serialize and break HMAC

assertions.name: calls-create-webhook
assertions.description: Code calls TurboWebhooks.create_webhook with urls (HTTPS) and events

assertions.name: router-wired
assertions.description: app/main.py was modified to include the new webhook router

assertions.name: env-has-webhook-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, and TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET

assertions.name: no-sender-env-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

evals.prompt: Wire up TurboDocx signature webhooks in my Laravel app. I need a public POST route to receive event deliveries (with HMAC verification on the X-TurboDocx-Signature header) and an admin endpoint that registers the org's signature webhook.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboWebhooks PHP config/service (admin-scoped, no sender email), a Laravel controller for the webhook receiver that reads $request->getContent() (raw body string) and calls verifyWebhookSignature, plus a registration endpoint that calls TurboWebhooks::createWebhook and returns the secret. Adds .env with TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboWebhooks config or service file was created

assertions.name: config-uses-webhooks-configure
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboWebhooks::configure (or TurboWebhooks::configureFromCredentials) with apiKey and orgId

assertions.name: no-sender-email-in-config
assertions.description: TurboWebhooks configure call does NOT pass senderEmail/senderName

assertions.name: controller-created
assertions.description: A Laravel controller was created for the webhook receiver

assertions.name: uses-raw-body
assertions.description: Receiver reads the raw body via $request->getContent() — NOT $request->json() / $request->all() / $request->validate(), which would parse and re-serialize

assertions.name: calls-verifyWebhookSignature
assertions.description: Receiver calls TurboWebhooks::verifyWebhookSignature (or verifyWebhookSignature helper) with the raw body, X-TurboDocx-Signature header, X-TurboDocx-Timestamp header, and the secret

assertions.name: calls-createWebhook
assertions.description: Code calls TurboWebhooks::createWebhook with urls and events

assertions.name: routes-defined
assertions.description: Webhook receiver and admin routes are registered in routes/api.php using Route::post

assertions.name: uses-laravel-patterns
assertions.description: Generated code uses Laravel patterns (Controller class, Request injection, response()->json())

assertions.name: env-has-webhook-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, and TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET

assertions.name: no-sender-env-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

evals.prompt: Add TurboDocx signature webhooks to my Go service (using net/http or Gin). I need a handler that receives the webhook POST and verifies the X-TurboDocx-Signature header, plus a CLI/admin path to register the signature webhook with TurboDocx.
evals.expected_output: Creates a Go config building a *WebhooksClient with NewWebhooksClientWithConfig (admin-scoped, NO SenderEmail), a handler that calls io.ReadAll(r.Body) and turbodocx.VerifyWebhookSignature before trusting the payload, and an admin call to wh.CreateWebhook that captures the Secret. Adds .env with TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A Go config/client file was created for TurboWebhooks

assertions.name: config-uses-NewWebhooksClientWithConfig
assertions.description: Config file calls turbodocx.NewWebhooksClientWithConfig — NOT NewClientWithConfig (which is for TurboSign and requires SenderEmail)

assertions.name: no-sender-email-in-config
assertions.description: Config does NOT pass SenderEmail/SenderName to the webhooks client (the webhooks constructor specifically skips that validation)

assertions.name: handler-file-created
assertions.description: A handler file was created for the inbound webhook receiver

assertions.name: uses-raw-body
assertions.description: Handler reads the raw body via io.ReadAll(r.Body) — NOT json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(...) before verification

assertions.name: calls-VerifyWebhookSignature
assertions.description: Handler calls turbodocx.VerifyWebhookSignature (the free function) with the raw body, signature header, timestamp header, secret, and options

assertions.name: no-decode-before-verify
assertions.description: Code does NOT call json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(...) or json.Unmarshal on r.Body BEFORE VerifyWebhookSignature — that loses whitespace and breaks HMAC

assertions.name: calls-CreateWebhook
assertions.description: Code calls wh.CreateWebhook with a CreateWebhookRequest containing HTTPS URLs and events

assertions.name: captures-secret
assertions.description: Code surfaces or prints created.Secret (shown ONCE — must be captured)

assertions.name: main-wired
assertions.description: cmd/server/main.go was modified to register the webhook handler

assertions.name: env-has-webhook-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, and TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET

assertions.name: no-sender-env-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

evals.prompt: Hook up TurboDocx signature webhooks in my Spring Boot app. I need a controller that receives the inbound POST and verifies the X-TurboDocx-Signature header before trusting the payload, plus a Spring service that can register the webhook with TurboDocx on demand.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboWebhooks Spring service that uses TurboDocxClient.Builder.buildWebhooksClient() (no senderEmail), a @RestController whose handler binds @RequestBody byte[] rawBody and calls WebhookSignatureVerifier.verify, and a register endpoint/service method that calls webhooks.createWebhook and returns the secret. Adds application.properties (or .env) with TURBODOCX_API_KEY / TURBODOCX_ORG_ID / TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboWebhooks Spring service/config file was created (e.g. TurboWebhooksService.java or TurboDocxWebhooksConfig.java)

assertions.name: config-uses-buildWebhooksClient
assertions.description: Config uses new TurboDocxClient.Builder()...buildWebhooksClient() — NOT .build() (which requires senderEmail for TurboSign)

assertions.name: no-sender-email-in-config
assertions.description: Config does NOT call .senderEmail(...) on the builder when constructing the webhooks client

assertions.name: controller-created
assertions.description: A Spring @RestController was created for the inbound webhook receiver

assertions.name: uses-raw-body
assertions.description: Receiver handler binds @RequestBody byte[] (or HttpServletRequest with getInputStream().readAllBytes()) — NOT a parsed Map/DTO @RequestBody

assertions.name: calls-WebhookSignatureVerifier-verify
assertions.description: Receiver calls WebhookSignatureVerifier.verify(rawBody, signatureHeader, timestampHeader, secret) — the static method on the final utility class

assertions.name: no-map-or-dto-binding
assertions.description: Receiver does NOT bind @RequestBody Map<String, Object> or @RequestBody SomeDto before verification — Jackson would re-serialize and break HMAC

assertions.name: calls-createWebhook
assertions.description: A register endpoint or service method calls webhooks.createWebhook with HTTPS urls and an events list

assertions.name: surfaces-secret
assertions.description: Response or returned value exposes the secret field from the createWebhook JsonObject (shown ONCE)

assertions.name: uses-spring-annotations
assertions.description: Generated code uses Spring annotations (@RestController, @PostMapping, @RequestHeader, @Bean or @Service)

assertions.name: env-has-webhook-vars
assertions.description: application.properties or .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY, TURBODOCX_ORG_ID, and TURBODOCX_WEBHOOK_SECRET (or turbodocx.api-key / turbodocx.org-id / turbodocx.webhook.secret equivalents)

assertions.name: no-sender-env-vars
assertions.description: Config does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL / turbodocx.sender-email (TurboWebhooks does not send mail)

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: Config does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

assertions.name: follows-java-conventions
assertions.description: Files placed in proper Java package directory structure (src/main/java/...)

evals.prompt: Add an endpoint to my Express app that converts HTML to DOCX documents. I want to POST HTML and get back a downloadable .docx file.
evals.expected_output: Creates a helper module wrapping HTMLtoDOCX, a route file with POST endpoint, wires route into main app, installs @turbodocx/html-to-docx

assertions.name: package-installed
assertions.description: package.json contains @turbodocx/html-to-docx as a dependency

assertions.name: helper-created
assertions.description: A helper/utility module was created that wraps HTMLtoDOCX

assertions.name: helper-imports-lib
assertions.description: Helper file imports HTMLtoDOCX from @turbodocx/html-to-docx

assertions.name: route-created
assertions.description: A route/handler file was created for the docx generation endpoint

assertions.name: correct-content-type
assertions.description: Response sets Content-Type to application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document

assertions.name: content-disposition
assertions.description: Response sets Content-Disposition header for file download

assertions.name: route-wired
assertions.description: Main app file (src/index.ts) was modified to import and register the new route

assertions.name: uses-typescript
assertions.description: Generated files use .ts extension (tsconfig.json was detected)

assertions.name: no-env-vars
assertions.description: No .env file created with API keys (this library doesn't need them)

evals.prompt: Add an API route to my Next.js app that accepts HTML in the request body and returns a generated DOCX file. I'm using the App Router.
evals.expected_output: Creates a helper module wrapping HTMLtoDOCX, an App Router API route in app/api/ with POST handler, installs @turbodocx/html-to-docx

assertions.name: package-installed
assertions.description: package.json contains @turbodocx/html-to-docx as a dependency

assertions.name: helper-created
assertions.description: A helper/utility module was created that wraps HTMLtoDOCX

assertions.name: helper-imports-lib
assertions.description: Helper file imports HTMLtoDOCX from @turbodocx/html-to-docx

assertions.name: api-route-created
assertions.description: API route created in app/api/ directory using route.ts convention

assertions.name: exports-post
assertions.description: API route exports a POST function (Next.js App Router pattern)

assertions.name: correct-content-type
assertions.description: Response includes Content-Type for DOCX (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)

assertions.name: uses-response
assertions.description: API route uses Response or NextResponse (not Express res)

assertions.name: no-app-wiring
assertions.description: No attempt to wire routes into a main app file (Next.js uses file-based routing)

evals.prompt: Create a Node.js script in my project that converts an HTML string to a DOCX file. I want a reusable function I can call from other scripts.
evals.expected_output: Creates a reusable module exporting a function that wraps HTMLtoDOCX, a demo script that generates a .docx file, installs @turbodocx/html-to-docx

assertions.name: package-installed
assertions.description: package.json contains @turbodocx/html-to-docx as a dependency

assertions.name: script-created
assertions.description: A script or module file was created for DOCX generation

assertions.name: imports-lib
assertions.description: Script imports HTMLtoDOCX from @turbodocx/html-to-docx

assertions.name: exports-function
assertions.description: Script exports a reusable function (not just inline code)

assertions.name: writes-file
assertions.description: Script writes the buffer to a .docx file using fs.writeFileSync or fs.writeFile

assertions.name: has-example
assertions.description: Script includes example HTML content to demonstrate usage

assertions.name: uses-async
assertions.description: Script uses async/await (HTMLtoDOCX is async)

evals.prompt: Add TurboQuote to my Express app so I can create a sales quote, add product line items, and send it to a customer for review. I want an endpoint that takes a company/contact + a list of products and returns the sent quote.
evals.expected_output: Creates a TurboQuote config (apiKey + orgId, NO senderEmail), a route/handler that creates a quote, adds product line items, and sends it for review (plus optionally downloads the quote PDF), wires it into the app, and adds .env with TURBODOCX_API_KEY + TURBODOCX_ORG_ID only — no sender email, no partner vars. Uses only real TurboQuote methods (JavaScript/TypeScript naming); no workflow/approval calls.

assertions.name: config-file-created
assertions.description: A TurboQuote config/client file was created

assertions.name: config-uses-turboquote
assertions.description: Config file calls TurboQuote.configure with apiKey and orgId — NOT TurboSign.configure

assertions.name: no-sender-email-in-config
assertions.description: TurboQuote is configured WITHOUT senderEmail/senderName — quotes are not signature emails and the SDK skips that validation

assertions.name: quote-route-created
assertions.description: An HTTP route/handler file was created for quote operations

assertions.name: creates-and-sends-quote
assertions.description: Route creates a quote (TurboQuote.createQuote), adds line items (TurboQuote.addLineItems), and sends it (TurboQuote.sendQuote)

assertions.name: routes-wired
assertions.description: The main app file was modified to register the quote route(s)

assertions.name: env-has-quote-vars
assertions.description: .env contains TURBODOCX_API_KEY and TURBODOCX_ORG_ID

assertions.name: no-sender-env-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_SENDER_EMAIL (TurboQuote does not send mail — common confusion with TurboSign)

assertions.name: no-partner-vars
assertions.description: .env does NOT contain TURBODOCX_PARTNER_API_KEY

assertions.name: no-workflow-approval
assertions.description: Code does NOT reference workflow/approval methods (createWorkflow, approveQuote, etc.) — these are not part of the TurboQuote SDK

assertions.name: gitignore-has-env
assertions.description: .gitignore includes .env
